summary: Add rows.Err() checks after every for rows.Next() iteration in novel commands so mid-iteration SQLite errors don't silently truncate results.
reason: Greptile P1 review of PR #635 flagged three sites (drift, quota_burn, watch.check) where the rows cursor was iterated and closed without consulting rows.Err(). The same omission existed at two more sites the review noted as parity (watch.list, history-cache) and one not in the review (log.show). All six now wrap-and-return the iterator error so partial datasets don't quietly drive drift rankings, burn-rate projections, threshold-crossing detection, or log queries.
validated_outcome: go build ./... clean; go test ./internal/cli passes; manual error injection verified via grep: every for rows.Next() loop in internal/cli/ is followed by an if err := rows.Err() guard.
